mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters efficiently. It automates the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ clusters, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to simplify RabbitMQ deployments in Kubernetes environments while ensuring high availability and scalability.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in a new namespace called rabbitmq-system.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ Cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ Cluster is Running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Lists all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process for upgrading the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Backs up existing RabbitMQ cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ pods may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: RabbitMQ cluster not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and network policies allow traffic.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for RabbitMQ.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the RabbitMQ cluster configuration and redeploy.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, added new metrics for monitoring.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
